questions.question: Find the syntax error in this code.
questions.explanation: Line 2 is missing a semicolon after the assignment. It should be `$name = "John";`.

questions.question: Identify the line that causes an error.
questions.codeSnippet:
<?php
$age = 25;
print "Age: $age";
print "Next year you will be " . $age + 1;
?>
questions.explanation: Line 3 uses `+` with concatenation incorrectly. The dot has higher precedence, so `"Next year you will be " . $age` becomes a string, then trying to add 1 causes a type error. It should be `"Next year you will be " . ($age + 1)`.

questions.question: Find the bug in this variable usage.
questions.explanation: Variable names are case-sensitive. `$firstname` and `$lastname` are different from `$first_name` and `$last_name`. The correct variables are `$first_name` and `$last_name`.
